title: Pozoj's Plains Villages
description: Datapack that enhances village structures in Minecraft's plains biome.
body:
![Pozoj's Plains Villages](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6483fd8b8f8a3624e28306e26f23836d49559138.jpeg)

**Pozoj's Plains Villages is a datapack that brings fresh detail and atmosphere to villages in Minecraft's plains biome, while carefully preserving the original building sizes to stay true to the vanilla design.Each house, shop, and functional structure has been thoughtfully redesigned with more attention to detail, without increasing their dimensions.** 

Village architecture now features more varied roof shapes, realistic chimneys with smoke, improved gardens, and better-defined animal pens—adding life and structure to the settlements. Unlike standard villages, the interiors of these buildings are fully furnished and functional, offering players a more immersive and lived-in experience. 
Pozoj's Plains Villages brings a new level of atmosphere and style without compromising the core Minecraft aesthetic—perfect for players who want more beautiful villages that still feel like part of the original game.
It is compatible with worldgen datapacks and works seamlessly on Paper/Spigot servers.

Feel free to showcase this datapack in your videos if you provide a link to the Planet Minecraft page or Patreon page. You may also use this datapack on multiplayer servers if you credit me and include a link to the Planet Minecraft page or Patreon page.


Please note that this datapack is not for sale and may not be published or used in other projects as your own. 

**Older versions are available on my Patreon page.**


<details>
<summary>Requirements</summary>
Minecraft Java Edition 1.17-1.21.5
</details>


<details>
<summary>Installation</summary>

## Singleplayer
- Open Minecraft
- Create a new world and click on "More", "Data Packs"
- Drag the data pack into the Minecraft window
- Click "Yes" on the confirmation screen
- Move the data pack to the right-hand side of the screen by clicking the triangle on its icon
- Click "Done" and continue creating your world, then click on "Create New World"

## Multiplayer
- Download the data pack
- Open your server folder, then open the folder of the world
- Put the data pack into a folder named datapacks
- Restart your server and you can use your new datapack.
 
</details>
